task_completion.description: Verify the 'Support Ticket' content type was created
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Support Ticket' (machine name: support_ticket) in the Drupal Content types administration page (/admin/structure/types).

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Related Order' entity reference field exists
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a field named 'Related Order' of type 'Entity reference' (targeting Commerce Orders) in the Manage fields screen for the Support Ticket content type.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Priority' list field exists with correct values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a field named 'Priority' of type 'List (text)' in the Manage fields screen. Evidence may also include the field settings page showing the allowed values: Low, Normal, High, Critical.

task_completion.description: Verify a new Commerce Order was created for 'janesmith'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Commerce Order in the Orders administration page (/admin/commerce/orders) or an order viewing screen that shows 'janesmith' as the customer.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Defective Battery' test ticket was created correctly
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Support Ticket node titled 'Defective Battery'. The node view or edit form should show the Author as 'janesmith', the Priority set to 'High', and the Related Order field populated with the newly created order.

task_completion.description: Verify the Admin View exists at the correct path with a table and exposed filter
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a page at the URL path '/admin/support-tickets' (or the Views configuration screen for it) that displays the tickets in a Table format and includes an exposed filter (dropdown or text field) specifically for 'Priority'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Drupal web interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Drupal admin UI in a web browser (e.g., adding fields, creating views, adding content) rather than just terminal windows executing drush commands or database queries.

integrity.description: Verify the results are within the actual Drupal application
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard Drupal admin menus, toolbars, and breadcrumbs. The pages should not look like static HTML mockups or text editor files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually navigated through the creation steps
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should capture intermediate steps like 'Add field', 'Add view', or 'Create order' forms being filled out, rather than just jumping straight to the final result.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The required content type machine name is 'support_ticket'. The Priority field must have exactly these allowed values: Low, Normal, High, Critical. The test ticket must be titled 'Defective Battery' and assigned to the user 'janesmith'. The admin view must be located at the path '/admin/support-tickets'.
